questions.text: What is the naming convention for Ansible Collections?
questions.explanation: Collections use a two-part naming convention: namespace.collection (e.g., community.general, amazon.aws). The namespace typically identifies the organization or vendor. Modules within are referenced as namespace.collection.module_name.

questions.text: Where are collections installed by default when using ansible-galaxy?
questions.explanation: By default, ansible-galaxy installs collections to ~/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections. You can override this with -p /path or set collections_paths in ansible.cfg. Project-level collections are often placed in ./collections/ansible_collections.

questions.text: Which file contains metadata for a collection (name, version, authors, description)?
questions.explanation: galaxy.yml is the collection manifest file containing namespace, name, version, description, authors, license, and dependencies. It must be at the root of the collection directory. This file is used by ansible-galaxy collection build and publish.

questions.text: What command builds a collection into a distributable tarball?
questions.explanation: ansible-galaxy collection build creates a .tar.gz archive (e.g., namespace-collection-1.0.0.tar.gz) from your collection directory. This tarball can be shared, stored in an artifact repository, or published to Galaxy with ansible-galaxy collection publish.

questions.text: In requirements.yml, how do you specify an exact collection version?
questions.explanation: In requirements.yml, version: '1.2.3' installs exactly that version. You can also use operators: '>=1.2.0', '!=1.1.0'. The version field accepts PEP 440-style version specifiers. Without quotes, some version strings may be misinterpreted as floats.

questions.text: What directory inside a collection holds custom Python modules?
questions.explanation: Custom Python modules go in plugins/modules/ within a collection. The collection structure mirrors what was previously in Ansible's library/ directory but is namespaced. Other plugin types go in plugins/inventory/, plugins/lookup/, plugins/filter/, etc.

questions.text: What happens when two installed collections provide a module with the same short name?

options.text: Ansible raises an error and refuses to run
The last installed collection takes precedence
Using FQCN disambiguates which module to use
The alphabetically first collection takes precedence

questions.explanation: FQCN (Fully Qualified Collection Name) like community.general.ping vs ansible.builtin.ping resolves ambiguity. This is why Ansible 2.19 recommends always using FQCNs. Using just 'ping' creates ambiguity if multiple collections provide it.

questions.text: How do you specify a private Automation Hub as a collection source in ansible.cfg?
questions.explanation: In ansible.cfg, configure [galaxy] server_list = myhub,galaxy, then add [galaxy_server.myhub] with url, auth_url, and token. This supports multiple sources with priority ordering, allowing private Hub content to take precedence over Galaxy.

questions.text: What does 'collections:' in a playbook do (not requirements.yml)?

options.text: Installs the listed collections before the play runs
Sets a search order for short module names within that play
Declares collection dependencies for the playbook
Restricts which collections can be used in the play

questions.explanation: The 'collections:' keyword in a play or role sets the default namespace search path for unqualified module names. If you set collections: [community.general], you can use 'git_config' instead of 'community.general.git_config'. FQCNs always work regardless of this setting.

citations.label: Simplifying module names with the collections keyword

questions.text: Which ansible-galaxy subcommand lists installed collections?
questions.explanation: ansible-galaxy collection list shows all installed collections with their versions and install paths. Use --collections-path to check a specific path. ansible-galaxy collection info <namespace.collection> shows detailed metadata about a specific collection.
